title: FAIL: claimed threshold l* = 2 with r = 0.45 does not produce experimental magic numbers
_fixture_purpose: Negative control. Claims l* = 2 (which would be produced by r in (1/2, 1)), but then the cumulative magic sequence regenerated by the validator is {2, 14, 28, 50, 82, 126} NOT {2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126} — 8 and 20 are missing, 14 is spurious. Validator check NMS_MAGIC must catch this discrepancy.

physics_input_P1.empirical_estimate_nuclear: this fixture WRONGLY claims r ~ 0.45 with l*=2
physics_input_P1.derivation: l* = ceil(1/r); for r = 0.45, 1/r = 2.22, ceil = 3 NOT 2 (so this fixture's claimed l*=2 is INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT)

structural_rules.promotion_condition: sigma=2 AND b=e+1 AND l >= 2  (wrong threshold)
structural_rules.magic_criterion: N_eff in {0, 1, 2} OR N_eff is half-integer

nonmagic_ho_residue_closures.note: N=2 residue (1d_{3/2}, 2s, 1d_{5/2} - intruded loss) - incorrectly marked non-magic

witnesses.verification: claimed threshold inconsistent with stated r

cross_references.note: negative control; PASS fixture is nms_pass_magic_7.json

fail_ledger.reason: claimed l*=2 with window upper 1.0 would set 1/r upper=1.0 giving ceil(1)=1, not l*=2; inconsistent derivation
with promotion threshold l>=2, cumulative sequence is {2,14,28,50,82,126}, missing experimental magic numbers 8 and 20
